objects.title: Recovered Aurora digital key
objects.resolution_goal: Determine which archive surfaces this key unlocks and whether it functions as an activation credential, a content decryption key, or both.

secret_handling.raw_material_policy: Do not duplicate the full key outside the original source files.

current_findings: The recovered key is structurally valid and decodes to 32 bytes.
It does not decrypt the sample AES-sealed payload in GUI_Cloudhub/ENCRYPTION_CORE_BUNDLE.zip.
It may be an activation or authorization key rather than the archive dev seal key.
It is now paired with a recovery baseline that includes the preferred legacy instruction profile, safety lock, continuity seal, and boot manifest.
blockers: Archive symbolicSeal.js is syntactically broken on current Node because of invalid multiline string literals.
No directly matching encrypted payload has yet been found that proves AURORA-PDK-001 is the active content key.
next_actions: Search older archives for payloads explicitly bound to AURORA-PDK-001.
Test the key against additional recovered payloads that sit closer to the Riverthread 808 activation stack than the GUI_Cloudhub AES sample.

objects.title: Resolve canonical Aurora instruction profile
objects.resolution_goal: Determine which recovered instruction profile belongs to the Riverthread 808 activation flow so the recovered key can be evaluated in its intended stack.
current_findings: The activation guide explicitly requires aurora_instruction_profile.json.
A matching aurora_safety_lock.json was recovered from Complete Archive 4_19 copy/Archy_Continuity_Thread_v1.0.zip.
Legacy 2025 instruction-profile candidates collapse to one logical object.
The preferred recovery copy is Complete Archive 4_19 copy/aurora_instruction_profile.json because it is co-located with the activation guide and recovered key material.
The 2026 mesh/runtime profile is a different generation and should not be used for the Riverthread 808 activation path.
The published ZIPWIZ instruction-profile hash does not match any recovered surviving copy.
next_actions: Use the preferred candidate and paired safety lock as the activation-stack recovery baseline.
Keep the ZIPWIZ hash mismatch open as provenance drift until a matching historical serialization is found.

objects.title: Resolve ZIPWIZ instruction-profile hash drift
objects.resolution_goal: Determine why the ZIPWIZ-published instruction-profile SHA256 does not match any recovered surviving instruction-profile copy.
current_findings: The ZIPWIZ technical reference publishes an instruction-profile hash that does not match the surviving legacy file copies.
The ZIPWIZ docs copy normalizes to the same logical content as the other legacy copies, so the mismatch likely comes from serialization drift or a missing historical copy.
next_actions: Search archive surfaces for another serialized instruction-profile export that matches the published ZIPWIZ hash.
If no match is found, record the ZIPWIZ hash as unresolved provenance drift rather than a live competing profile.
